sections.heading: Alert Conditions
sections.body:
To trigger alerts, you can configure the following alert conditions in
the project-level [Configure Alert Settings](configure-alerts) page.

- [Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling initiated for base tier](<Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling initiated for base tier>)
  occurs if Atlas starts compute auto-scaling for any of the [operational node](manual/reference/glossary)
  in your dedicated cluster. Atlas can scale disk capacity as part
  of this event.

- [Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling initiated for analytics tier](<Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling initiated for analytics tier>)
  occurs if Atlas starts compute auto-scaling for any of
  the [analytics node](manual/reference/glossary) in your dedicated cluster.
  Atlas can scale disk capacity as part of this event.

- [Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling down didn't initiate for base tier due to storage requirements](<Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling down didn't initiate for base tier due to storage requirements>)
  occurs if Atlas couldn't start compute auto-scaling for any of
  the [operational node](manual/reference/glossary) in your dedicated cluster
  as the configured storage size isn't supported by the target cluster tier.

- [Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling down didn't initiate for analytics tier due to storage requirements](<Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling down didn't initiate for analytics tier due to storage requirements>)
  occurs if Atlas couldn't start compute auto-scaling for any of
  the [analytics node](manual/reference/glossary) in your dedicated cluster
  as the configured storage size isn't supported by the target cluster tier.

- [Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling didn't initiate for base tier due to maximum configured cluster tier](<Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling didn't initiate for base tier due to maximum configured cluster tier>)
  occurs if Atlas couldn't scale up an [operational node](manual/reference/glossary)
  because your cluster reached a maximum cluster tier configured for auto-scaling.

- [Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling didn't initiate for analytics tier due to maximum configured cluster tier](<Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling didn't initiate for analytics tier due to maximum configured cluster tier>)
  occurs if Atlas couldn't scale up an [analytics node](manual/reference/glossary)
  because your cluster reached a maximum cluster tier configured for auto-scaling.

- [Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling didn't initiate for base tier due to insufficient oplog size](<Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling didn't initiate for base tier due to insufficient oplog size>)
  occurs if Atlas couldn't scale up an [operational node](manual/reference/glossary)
  due to insufficient [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) size. To learn more, see [minimum retention window for Oplog entries](set-oplog-min-window).

- [Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling didn't initiate for analytics tier due to insufficient oplog size](<Auto-scaling: Compute auto-scaling didn't initiate for analytics tier due to insufficient oplog size>)
  occurs if Atlas couldn't scale up an [analytics node](manual/reference/glossary)
  due to insufficient [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) size. To learn more, see [minimum retention window for Oplog entries](set-oplog-min-window).

- [Auto-scaling: Predictive compute auto-scaling initiated for base tier](<Auto-scaling: Predictive compute auto-scaling initiated for base tier>)
  occurs if Atlas starts predictive compute auto-scaling for any of the
  [operational node](manual/reference/glossary) in your dedicated cluster.

- [Auto-scaling: Predictive compute auto-scaling did not initiate for base tier due to maximum configured cluster tier](<Auto-scaling: Predictive compute auto-scaling did not initiate for base tier due to maximum configured cluster tier>)
  occurs if Atlas couldn't predictively scale up an [operational node](manual/reference/glossary)
  because your cluster reached a maximum cluster tier configured for auto-scaling.

- [Auto-scaling: Predictive auto-scaling did not initiate for base tier due to insufficient oplog size](<Auto-scaling: Predictive auto-scaling did not initiate for base tier due to insufficient oplog size>)
  occurs if Atlas couldn't predictively scale up an [operational node](manual/reference/glossary)
  due to insufficient [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) size. To learn more, see [minimum retention window for Oplog entries](set-oplog-min-window).

- [Auto-scaling: Disk auto-scaling initiated](<Auto-scaling: Disk auto-scaling initiated>)
  occurs when Atlas starts auto-scaling disk capacity.

- [Auto-scaling: Disk auto-scaling didn't initiate due to the cluster reaching maximum available disk size](<Auto-scaling: Disk auto-scaling didn't initiate due to the cluster reaching maximum available disk size>)
  occurs if Atlas couldn't scale up the disk size because the cluster
  has reached maximum available disk size.

- [Auto-scaling: Disk auto-scaling didn't initiate due to insufficient oplog size](<Auto-scaling: Disk auto-scaling didn't initiate due to insufficient oplog size>)
  occurs if Atlas couldn't scale up the disk size because the cluster's oplog
  size isn't sufficient. To learn more, see [minimum retention window for Oplog entries](set-oplog-min-window).

sections.heading: Common Triggers
sections.body:
Auto-scaling alerts occur by default for your cluster when Atlas
triggers them. You can change who receives these alerts and their distribution.
To learn more about how auto-scaling behaves, see [Cluster Auto-Scaling](cluster-autoscaling).

Automatic scaling might not occur when a cluster receives a burst of activity,
such as a bulk insert. To reduce the risk of running out of resources during
automatic scaling up events, monitor your cluster during bulk inserts and
other workload spikes.

sections.heading: Fix the Immediate Problem
sections.body:
If your cluster nodes run out of memory or disk space, you can
[Modify the Cluster Tier](scale-cluster-instance) to fix the immediate
problem. You can select a cluster tier with more memory, storage, and IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second).
